message:
refactor(mux_client): extract PaneDelta channel helpers for reuse across subscription paths

Add four small helpers above PaneOutputSubscription that encapsulate
the common patterns for sending / receiving PaneDelta values through
the bounded mpsc channels that back every pane subscription. Purely
mechanical extraction — zero call-site migrations in this commit, so
no behavior change. The helpers are here for upcoming refactors that
will migrate the existing inline recv/send call sites to the shared
entry points so the cx-threading and reserve/send discipline is
enforced in one place rather than cargo-culted across the module.

## Helpers

- pane_delta_recv_with_cx(cx, rx) -> Option<PaneDelta>
  Canonical recv: takes explicit &Cx (runtime_compat requires it),
  returns Option to distinguish closed (None) from happy path.

- [cfg(test)] pane_delta_recv(rx) -> Option<PaneDelta>
  Test-only ergonomic wrapper that resolves ambient_mux_cx() and
  forwards. Gated on cfg(test) so the ambient-cx resolution doesn't
  slip into production.

- [cfg(test)] pane_delta_send(tx, delta)
  Test-only send wrapper around mpsc_reserve_send. Production code
  keeps using mpsc_reserve_send directly so backpressure signals
  aren't silently discarded.

- pane_delta_try_send(tx, delta) -> bool
  Non-blocking send on best-effort paths (pane-ended broadcasts
  where blocking would deadlock against subscription teardown).
  bool return surfaces whether the message was queued.

- pane_delta_try_emit_ended(tx, pane_id, reason)
  Sugar over pane_delta_try_send for the PaneDelta::Ended shape
  which recurs across shutdown / lost-connection / cancelled
  teardown paths. Return value ignored — emission is best-effort.

No call sites migrated. Migration is a follow-up that can be split
per-site or batched depending on review preferences.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
